title: Semiconductors | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
Moving talent with the right skills to the right locations at the right moment—and moving them out again—is an operation that requires flawless precision.
Multiple crises—from supply-chain bottlenecks, to international geopolitical conflict, to the global pandemic—have made that operation even more complex for the semiconductor industry, demanding ever-faster moves to reposition workers and preserve productivity.
In midst of all this complexity, we strive to embody the precision, persistence and commitment that sets the semiconductor industry apart. Fragomen helps manufacturers, suppliers and service providers respond to unexpected challenges, with our legal professionals helping to design immigration programs that attract talent, manage crisis and build value.

### Semiconductor Industry Experience
As a firm, our commitment to the semiconductor industry is second to none. We have a deep bench of Partners who are significantly invested in the sector—both at the policy level and with clients. Our dedicated Semiconductor Industry Practice Group meets on a bi-weekly basis to discuss industry trends, legal strategies and confidential industry benchmarking—all with the goal of ensuring our clients remain competitive amongst peers and that we are employing the very best immigration and global mobility strategies. We are also actively involved in the Global Semiconductor Alliance (GSA), the Semiconductor Industry Association (SIA) and Canada’s Semiconductor Council , which offers additional opportunities for our firm to stay abreast of key industry happenings.  By leveraging our experience and understanding of this industry, we can provide our clients with guidance on best practices in immigration policies and programs.

### Clients value us for:
Geopolitical and economic forces compel the industry to consider opportunities outside of traditional manufacturing centers. Fragomen’s presence in nearly every country and territory across the globe helps our clients make strategic moves quickly and efficiently.
From the largest industry players tackling ramps of hundreds of workers, to smaller manufacturers and suppliers seeking an edge through competitive immigration programs, every bracket of the industry relies on Fragomen for thoughtful advice tailored to their needs.
Supply Chain Coverage
We support the unique immigration needs of suppliers, manufacturers and everyone in between. Our speed, flexibility and hard-won experience allows us to extend programs to include our clients’ contractors and guest workers on a temporary or long-term basis.
Embedded Intelligence
Our client teams embed securely and seamlessly within our clients’ organizations, reducing administrative burdens and making immigration programs more efficient.
Respect for Authority
We negotiate with government agencies around the world—from national to municipal levels. Clients depend on the relationships we’ve formed with these government agencies to avoid bureaucratic obstacles, plan moves accurately and enrich the employee experience.
The world is an unpredictable place, roiled by conflict and threats to public health. We work tirelessly to help clients move their workforce out of harm’s way as quickly and safely as possible. Specifically, during the pandemic shutdown, we were instrumental in securing critical worker status for semiconductor industry personnel.
Our unique consular practice squeezes the most efficient results out of the most unpredictable part of the immigration process: consular review.
As a firm, our commitment to the semiconductor industry is second to none. We are a proud member of the Semiconductor Industry Association and the Global Semiconductor Alliance—leading voices in your industry. We have a deep bench of Partners who are significantly invested in the sector—both at the policy level and with clients. We partner with you to make your voice heard in the most consequential immigration debates. Our thought leadership has influenced decision-making in Washington and capitals around the world.
Strategic Advice and Policy Development
Market potential, tax policy, political stability—dozens of concerns play into cross-border business planning. Immigration policy is just as important. We ensure that clients don’t suffer unexpected surprises when they move their workforce to new jurisdictions.
Because we represent the lion’s share of players in the semiconductor industry, we have vast, sector-specific data at our disposal. Our benchmarking and trend analysis capabilities help our clients reach their goals—and imagine new ones.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
